text.title: Figma vs Adobe Strategy 2026 Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the figma-vs-adobe-strategy-2026 methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Decision MUST be scored on five axes: collaboration, asset generation, handoff, agent API surface, total cost.
rule.rationale: Single-axis decisions miss the cross-vendor strengths.

rule.statement: When agent API + commercial-safe assets are both required, the Figma + Firefly hybrid MUST be the default recommendation.
rule.rationale: No single 2026 vendor covers both well.

rule.statement: Decision MUST be revisited at most 6 months later.
rule.rationale: Both vendors ship major capability changes annually.

rule.statement: Recommendation MUST name the migration cost (component library port, training, license overlap) explicitly.
rule.rationale: Hidden migration cost is the usual reason decisions stall.

rule.statement: If agent automation is required, the recommendation MUST cite the specific REST endpoints used.
rule.rationale: Hand-wavy 'API support' claims hide where vendors are weak.

rule.statement: When preconditions hold, run the full procedure end-to-end and emit the output artefact.
rule.rationale: Routes positive decision-tree leaves to the procedure.

rule.statement: When preconditions fail or skip-conditions hold, exit without producing the artefact and document why.
rule.rationale: Routes negative decision-tree leaves out without polluting the output channel.
